text: The robotic fourth arm grasps the cornea incorporating the round ligament at a perpendicular angle, applying cranial superior traction to expose the triangle of freedom. The surgeon uses medial traction on the ovarian vessels to enter the retroperitoneum, extending the incision parallel to the ovarian vessels with a spread and lift technique. The dissection proceeds inferiorly through areolar tissue until the posterior peritoneum is visible. The surgeon uses a grasper with counter traction to push and spread, advancing towards the ureter. Upon identifying the ureter, the superior areolar tissue is gently dissected to clear the path, avoiding uterine vessel trauma.
